_doc: The 6 base face-turn generators for a 3x3 cube, in the cubie model (D1/D2).
Everything else (primes R', doubles R2, and the 18-move agent action space)
is DERIVED from these by core/moves.py -- you never hand-write an inverse.
Applying a move M to a state S produces S' by, for every slot i:
S'.cp[i] = S.cp[ cp[i] ]              corner in slot i is pulled from slot cp[i]
S'.co[i] = (S.co[ cp[i] ] + co[i]) % 3   ...and twisted by co[i]
Because applying a move to the SOLVED state (identity perm, zero orient)
just copies the tables out, each table below IS the cube state after that
single move from solved. That makes them easy to eyeball and to compose.
These are the canonical Kociemba tables. Do not trust them on sight --
tests/test_moves.py proves them via R^4=I, (R U R' U')^6=I, M.M'=I, and a
scramble/inverse round-trip. If you edit a table, the tests are the judge.
_ui_only_moves: Slices (M E S), wide turns (Rw Lw ...), and view rotations (x y z) are UI/
documentation concepts, NOT part of the agent action space. Slices move
centers and would break the O(1) solved-check; view rotations are camera-only
and never mutate state. They are intentionally absent here in Phase 1 and
will be added as a separate, clearly-flagged section when the web layer needs
them -- adding one is a JSON edit, not a code change.
